label: Third Hour (Tertia)
description: The Byzantine Third Hour is sung at the third hour of the day. It consists of fixed psalmody (Psalms 16, 24, 50 LXX), the Trisagion prayer sequence, a troparion of the day, the Theotokion of the hour, and the Prayer of the Third Hour.

sections.label: Opening
sections.notes: The Third Hour opens with the Usual Beginning, as do all Byzantine offices.

items.text: The Priest blesses: Blessed is our God, always, now and ever and unto ages of ages.

items.label: The Usual Beginning
items.note: Trisagion prayers sequence. Resolved from third-hour-fixed.json by HorologionEngine.

sections.label: Fixed Psalmody
sections.notes: The Third Hour appoints three fixed psalms: 16, 24, and 50 (LXX). These are invariable on ordinary days.

items.label: Psalm 16 (LXX) — Hear, O Lord, my righteousness
Psalm 24 (LXX) — To Thee, O Lord, have I lifted up my soul
Psalm 50 (LXX) — Have mercy on me, O God

sections.label: Trisagion Prayers
sections.notes: The Trisagion prayer sequence precedes the troparia.

sections.label: Troparia
sections.notes: The dismissal troparion of the day is sung. The Theotokion of the Third Hour follows. On ferial days the troparion is from the Octoechos or Menaion; on Sundays the Resurrectional Troparion of the current tone.

items.label: Troparion of the Day
items.note: Variable troparion: resolved via Menaion resolver or weekday-theme rubric by HorologionEngine. Same infrastructure as Vespers and First Hour troparion slot.

items.label: Theotokion of the Third Hour
items.note: Variable: tone-dependent Theotokion from the Octoechos Third Hour cycle. Resolved by HorologionEngine as explicit rubric stub at baseline; full text deferred pending Third Hour Octoechos data.

sections.label: Prayer of the Third Hour and Dismissal
sections.notes: The Prayer of the Third Hour concludes the fixed psalmody cycle. The office ends with the standard dismissal.

items.label: Prayer of the Third Hour
items.note: Fixed invariable prayer. Resolved from third-hour-fixed.json by HorologionEngine.

items.text:
Priest: Christ our true God, through the intercessions of His all-pure Mother, of all the Saints, have mercy on us and save us, as He is good and the Lover of mankind.
Choir: Amen.
